source_pr.title: statistics: add GC for mysql.column_stats_usage
source_pr.body:
### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #29182

### What is changed and how it works?

Implement GC for `mysql.column_stats_usage`.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- bugfix or new feature needs a release note -->

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: tk.MustQuery(fmt.Sprintf("select is_index, hist_id, stats_ver from mysql.stats_histograms where table_id = %d and stats_ver > 0 order by is_index, hist_id", t1.Meta().ID)).Check( (executor/analyze_test.go)
failure_signature: tk.MustQuery(fmt.Sprintf("select is_index, hist_id, stats_ver from mysql.stats_histograms where table_id = %d and stats_ver > 0 order by is_index, hist_id", t1.Meta().ID)).Check( (executor/analyze_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: tk.MustQuery(fmt.Sprintf("select is_index, hist_id, stats_ver from mysql.stats_histograms where table_id = %d and stats_ver > 0 order by is_index, hist_id", t1.Meta().ID)).Check( (executor/analyze_test.go).
fix_pattern: tk.MustQuery(fmt.Sprintf("select is_index, hist_id, stats_ver from mysql.stats_histograms where table_id = %d and stats_ver > 0 order by is_index, hist_id", t1.Meta().ID)).Check( (executor/analyze_test.go)

evidence: evidence: tk.MustQuery(fmt.Sprintf("select is_index, hist_id, stats_ver from mysql.stats_histograms where table_id = %d and stats_ver > 0 order by is_index, hist_id", t1.Meta().ID)).Check( (executor/analyze_test.go)

files: errno/errcode.go, errno/errname.go, errors.toml, executor/analyze.go, executor/analyze_test.go, executor/builder.go, planner/core/errors.go, planner/core/errors_test.go, planner/core/planbuilder.go, statistics/handle/ddl.go, statistics/handle/gc.go, statistics/handle/gc_test.go, statistics/handle/handle.go, statistics/handle/handle_test.go
